_comment: Atomic backlog for the DreamForgeIdeas example app. See example-app-requirements.md for the full spec. Use scripts/load-example-features.mjs to insert these into a project's backlog. Descriptions are intentionally prescriptive — they list the exact files to write/edit so smaller models that tend to read-but-not-write get a clear nudge toward actual implementation.

features.title: Scaffold Next.js app
features.description:
ACTION: scaffold a Next.js 16 App Router project IN THE CURRENT WORKING DIRECTORY (your shell cwd IS the project root — do NOT cd elsewhere, do NOT use absolute paths).

STEPS YOU MUST EXECUTE:
1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --use-npm --yes --skip-install` via Bash. The `.` makes it scaffold INTO cwd. If it complains the directory is not empty, mv any conflicting hidden folders aside, then retry.
2. Run `npm install` via Bash to install dependencies.
3. Use Read on `package.json` to verify `next`, `react`, `react-dom`, `tailwindcss` are listed.

Do NOT just plan or describe — EXECUTE the bash commands. The session is incomplete unless `package.json` and `app/` exist on disk afterwards.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- package.json lists next, react, react-dom, tailwindcss
- app/page.tsx and app/layout.tsx exist
- node_modules/ exists (npm install ran)
- npm run dev would listen on http://localhost:3000

features.title: Add SQLite and Drizzle ORM
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- lib/db.ts exists and exports a `db` Drizzle instance
- drizzle.config.ts exists and references ./lib/schema.ts and ./drizzle
- package.json now lists better-sqlite3, drizzle-orm, drizzle-kit, @types/better-sqlite3
- node_modules/better-sqlite3 exists

features.title: Define ideas schema and migrate
features.description:
ACTION: define the `ideas` table schema and run the initial migration.

FILES YOU MUST CREATE OR MODIFY (use Write):
- `lib/schema.ts` — defines the `ideas` table with these columns:
  ```ts
  import { sqliteTable, integer, text } from 'drizzle-orm/sqlite-core';
  import { sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
  export const ideas = sqliteTable('ideas', {
    id: integer('id').primaryKey({ autoIncrement: true }),
    name: text('name').notNull(),
    description: text('description'),
    status: text('status').notNull().default('idea'),
    githubUrl: text('github_url'),
    localRepoPath: text('local_repo_path'),
    productionUrl: text('production_url'),
    marketSector: text('market_sector'),
    progressPercent: integer('progress_percent').notNull().default(0),
    createdAt: text('created_at').notNull().default(sql`CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`),
    updatedAt: text('updated_at').notNull().default(sql`CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`),
  });
  ```

BASH COMMANDS YOU MUST RUN:
- `npx drizzle-kit generate` — produces a SQL migration in ./drizzle/.
- `npx drizzle-kit migrate` — applies it, creating data.sqlite.

The session is incomplete unless lib/schema.ts exists, ./drizzle/ contains a *.sql migration file, and data.sqlite exists at the project root.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- lib/schema.ts exists with all 11 columns of the ideas table
- ./drizzle/ directory contains at least one .sql migration file
- data.sqlite exists at the project root
- A no-op `npx drizzle-kit generate` afterwards reports no schema changes

features.title: List and create ideas API
features.description:
ACTION: add the GET / POST API for ideas.

FILE YOU MUST CREATE (use Write):
- `app/api/ideas/route.ts` — exports two named functions:
  - `GET()` returns `NextResponse.json({ ideas: <all rows> })` by querying `db.select().from(ideas)`.
  - `POST(request)` parses the JSON body, validates `name` is a non-empty string (returns 400 otherwise), inserts a row with `db.insert(ideas).values({ name, description?, status?, ... }).returning().get()`, returns 201 with the inserted row. Default status to 'idea' and progressPercent to 0 if not provided.

Imports you'll need: `NextResponse` from 'next/server', `db` from '@/lib/db', `ideas` from '@/lib/schema'.

The session is incomplete unless app/api/ideas/route.ts exists on disk.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- app/api/ideas/route.ts exists and exports GET + POST
- Hitting GET /api/ideas returns { ideas: [] } on a fresh DB
- POST /api/ideas with {"name":"x"} returns 201 with the inserted row
- POST without a name returns 400

features.title: Update and delete ideas API
features.description:
ACTION: add the PATCH / DELETE API for individual ideas.

FILE YOU MUST CREATE (use Write):
- `app/api/ideas/[id]/route.ts` — exports two named functions:
  - `PATCH(request, { params })` parses `params.id` as integer, parses the JSON body, builds a partial update with only the fields present in the body, refreshes `updatedAt` to `new Date().toISOString()`, runs `db.update(ideas).set(patch).where(eq(ideas.id, id)).returning().get()`. Returns the row, or 404 if no row matched.
  - `DELETE(_, { params })` runs `db.delete(ideas).where(eq(ideas.id, id)).returning().get()`. Returns `{ success: true }`, or 404 if nothing was deleted.

Imports: `NextResponse`, `db`, `ideas`, `eq` from 'drizzle-orm'. In Next 16, `params` is a Promise — `await ctx.params` first.

The session is incomplete unless app/api/ideas/[id]/route.ts exists on disk.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- app/api/ideas/[id]/route.ts exists and exports PATCH + DELETE
- PATCH on a nonexistent id returns 404
- DELETE on a nonexistent id returns 404
- A successful DELETE removes the row from subsequent GET /api/ideas results

features.title: Layout shell with sidebar
features.description:
ACTION: replace the default app/layout.tsx body with a sidebar + main content shell. FILE YOU MUST EDIT (use Edit, NOT Write — keep the existing imports + metadata + html/body wrapper): - `app/layout.tsx` — inside the `` element, render: ```tsx
{children}
``` - Buttons have NO click handlers yet — visual shell only. The session is incomplete unless app/layout.tsx now contains the sidebar markup. Reading it without editing it does NOT complete this feature.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- app/layout.tsx renders a flex container with a 240px-wide sidebar and a main content area
- Sidebar contains 'Add New Idea' and 'View All Ideas' buttons
- Main background is bg-stone-50, sidebar is bg-stone-100
- The layout still wraps `{children}` so child routes render inside <main>

features.title: Ideas table UI
features.description: ACTION: replace app/page.tsx with a client component that fetches /api/ideas and renders a table. FILE YOU MUST WRITE (overwriting the create-next-app default): - `app/page.tsx` — starts with `"use client";`, uses React.useEffect to fetch /api/ideas on mount, stores the result in state, renders a `` with the columns: name, status, marketSector, progressPercent, githubUrl, productionUrl. URLs render as `{...}`. Progress shown as `${row.progressPercent}%`. Show 'No ideas yet' when the array is empty. Use Tailwind classes for table styling (`min-w-full`, `text-sm`, `border-collapse`, `text-left p-2`, etc.). The session is incomplete unless app/page.tsx no longer matches the create-next-app default and instead renders the ideas table.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- app/page.tsx is a client component ("use client" directive)
- Fetches /api/ideas on mount
- Renders a table with the 6 specified columns
- Empty state: 'No ideas yet'
- githubUrl and productionUrl render as anchor tags with target=_blank and rel=noreferrer

features.title: Add idea modal
features.description:
ACTION: build an Add Idea modal and wire it to the sidebar button.

FILES YOU MUST CREATE OR EDIT:
- `components/AddIdeaModal.tsx` (use Write) — a client component that renders a fixed-position overlay with a form. Form fields: name (required text), description (textarea), status (select: idea/building/launched), githubUrl (text), localRepoPath (text), productionUrl (text), marketSector (text), progressPercent (number 0-100). On submit, POST to /api/ideas with the form data; on success call props.onAdded() and props.onClose(). Validate that `name` is non-empty before submit; if empty show an inline error and do not POST. ESC key dismisses (use a useEffect with keydown listener).
- `app/page.tsx` (use Edit) — add a state `showAddModal`, render `<AddIdeaModal open={showAddModal} onClose={...} onAdded={refetch} />`.
- `app/layout.tsx` (use Edit) — extract the 'Add New Idea' button into something the page can listen to, OR move the button + modal into a shared client component. Simplest path: turn the layout's sidebar into a client component that exposes the open-modal action via a context or a simple window event.

The session is incomplete unless components/AddIdeaModal.tsx exists AND the page can open it.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- components/AddIdeaModal.tsx exists and renders a form covering all 8 fields
- Clicking 'Add New Idea' opens the modal
- ESC and a close button dismiss the modal
- Submitting with a name value adds a row to the table without a full page reload
- Submitting without a name shows an inline error and does NOT POST

features.title: Inline edit and delete
features.description:
ACTION: add per-row Edit and Delete buttons to the ideas table.

FILES YOU MUST EDIT (use Edit):
- `app/page.tsx` — add a final 'Actions' column to the table; each row gets two buttons: Edit and Delete.
  - Edit: opens the existing AddIdeaModal in edit mode (pre-populated with the row's current values). On save it must call PATCH /api/ideas/[id] with the changed fields, refresh the table, and close the modal.
  - Delete: calls `window.confirm('Delete this idea?')`. If confirmed, DELETE /api/ideas/[id], then refresh the table.
- `components/AddIdeaModal.tsx` — extend with an optional `initialValues` prop and an `editingId` prop. When editingId is set, the submit handler must PATCH /api/ideas/[editingId] instead of POSTing.

The session is incomplete unless the table has visible Edit and Delete buttons that call the API.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Each row has visible Edit and Delete buttons
- Edit pre-populates the modal with the row's current values
- Saving the edit issues a PATCH and the row reflects the new values without a page reload
- Delete prompts via window.confirm; cancelling does nothing
- Confirming Delete issues a DELETE and the row disappears

features.title: Sortable columns and search
features.description:
ACTION: add column sorting and a name/marketSector search input to the ideas table.

FILE YOU MUST EDIT (use Edit on app/page.tsx):
- Add local state: `sortKey` ('name' | 'status' | 'marketSector' | 'progressPercent' | null), `sortDir` ('asc' | 'desc'), and `searchQuery` (string).
- Above the table, render a search input bound to `searchQuery`. Filter rows by name OR marketSector using a case-insensitive substring match BEFORE sorting.
- Make name, status, marketSector, progressPercent column headers clickable — clicking toggles sortKey/sortDir. Render ▲ on the active column when sortDir=='asc' and ▼ when sortDir=='desc'. URL columns are NOT sortable.
- Apply sort + filter via React.useMemo so the table re-renders correctly.

The session is incomplete unless app/page.tsx now has both the search input AND clickable sort headers.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- A search input renders above the table
- Typing in it filters rows in real time (case-insensitive substring on name OR marketSector)
- Clicking a sortable header toggles asc → desc → asc and shows ▲ or ▼
- Search and sort compose: searching first, then sorting, keeps the filter active
- URL columns (githubUrl, productionUrl) are not sortable and do not show indicators
